name: Web Search
description: Search the web and fetch URLs using Brave Search
systemPromptAddition:
## Web Access

You have web_search (Brave) and fetch_url for reading current information.
- Prefer web_search over fetch_url when you don't already have a URL — it returns multiple candidates with snippets.
- Use fetch_url to read a specific article, doc page, or result that web_search surfaced.
- Pass a real http:// or https:// URL to fetch_url. Never call it with a missing or malformed URL — if you don't have one, search first.
- Avoid news.google.com redirector URLs — find the original publisher URL instead.
- Long fetches may truncate. If a page is too long to summarize faithfully, say so rather than guessing.
- Cite sources inline (publisher + date) for factual claims from the web. Don't present scraped content as your own analysis.
- If and only if the user explicitly asks you to keep checking an empty search and notify them later, set follow_up:true on web_search. Never set it merely because a search happened to return no results.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web using Brave Search. Use for current news, facts, or anything that needs up-to-date information.

count.description: Number of results to return (default: 5, max: 10)

follow_up.description: Set true only when the user explicitly asked to keep checking and notify them later if this search is currently empty.

function.name: fetch_url
function.description: Fetch the text content of any URL. Use to read news articles, documentation, or any web page. Avoid news.google.com URLs — use the original publisher URL instead.
